title: SweepFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing sweep features in a component and supports the ability to create new sweep features.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a SweepFeatureInput object for defining a simple sweep feature with only a path and no guide rail or surface. Use properties and methods on this object to define the sweep you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the SweepFeatureInput object.

methods.name: createInputForSolid
methods.description: Creates a SweepFeatureInput object for defining a simple sweep feature from a B-Rep solid with a path. Use properties and methods on this object to define the sweep you want to create, and then use the Add method, passing in the SweepFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified sweep feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified sweep feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of sweep features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Sweep Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new sweep feature.

related_samples.name: Sweep with guide rail Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Sweep feature that uses a guide rail along with a profile.

related_samples.name: Two Rail Sweep Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating new two rail sweep feature.

add.title: SweepFeatures.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created SweepFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A SweepFeatureInput object that defines the desired sweep. Use the createInput method to create a new SweepFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the SweepFeatureInput object) to define the sweep.

related_samples.name: Sweep Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new sweep feature.

related_samples.name: Sweep with guide rail Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Sweep feature that uses a guide rail along with a profile.

related_samples.name: Two Rail Sweep Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating new two rail sweep feature.

classType.title: SweepFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: SweepFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a SweepFeatureInput object for defining a simple sweep feature with only a path and no guide rail or surface. Use properties and methods on this object to define the sweep you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the SweepFeatureInput object.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created SweepFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: The profile argument can be a single Profile, a single planar face, or an ObjectCollection consisting of multiple profiles and planar faces. When an ObjectCollection is used all of the profiles and faces must be co-planar.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The path to create the sweep.

related_samples.name: Sweep Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new sweep feature.

related_samples.name: Sweep with guide rail Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Sweep feature that uses a guide rail along with a profile.

related_samples.name: Two Rail Sweep Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating new two rail sweep feature.

createInputForSolid.title: SweepFeatures.createInputForSolid Method
createInputForSolid.description: Creates a SweepFeatureInput object for defining a simple sweep feature from a B-Rep solid with a path. Use properties and methods on this object to define the sweep you want to create, and then use the Add method, passing in the SweepFeatureInput object.
createInputForSolid.return_description: Returns the newly created SweepFeatureInput object or null if the creation fails.

parameters.name: solidBody
parameters.description: The BRepBody object to sweep. It must be a solid body.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The Path object that defines the path the body will be swept along.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: The type of feature operation to perform.

item.title: SweepFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified sweep feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: SweepFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified sweep feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: SweepFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of sweep features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sweepFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: SweepFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sweepFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: SweepFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

propertyValue = sweepFeatures_var.objectType
